info.name: Teamoria API - v1
info.description:
Share-ready Teamoria API collection.

Quick start:
1. Import this collection and `Teamoria.local.postman_environment.json`.
2. Select the Teamoria Local environment and set `api_key`.
3. Authenticate with an administrator account before using the Admin folder.
4. Authenticate with a company account before using the Company folder.
5. A new company_owner must run Company > Onboarding > Register First Company before using company-scoped endpoints.
6. Admin project creation requires company_id; company project creation derives the company automatically.
7. Set project_member_user_id/task_assignee_id/upload_shared_user_id to users from the selected project's company, or let the create-user scripts populate them when empty.

Admin endpoints have global platform and billing access. Company endpoints remain isolated to the authenticated user's company. Integration scripts validate responses and store generated tokens and resource identifiers.

No production API keys, Sanctum tokens, or Google tokens are stored in this collection.

item.name: [0] System

request.description: Confirms that the API is reachable. This endpoint does not require an API key.

item.description: Public API health and operational endpoints.

item.name: [2] Admin
[2.1] Dashboard
item.description: Platform-wide administrator dashboard metrics and recent activity.

request.description: Returns platform-wide totals, project and task status distributions, five recent projects, and five upcoming tasks.

item.name: [2.2] Users

query.description: Set to true to return only soft-deleted users.

request.description: Returns the paginated user directory. Set archived=true to list soft-deleted users. Administrator access is required.

exec: const scope = pm.environment && pm.environment.name ? pm.environment : pm.collectionVariables;

request.description: Creates a company user and stores its identifier in managed_user_id. Valid roles: admin, company_owner, company_manager, company_member. Administrator access is required.
Returns the selected user. Administrator access is required.
Updates the selected user. Valid statuses: pending, active, suspended, inactive. Administrator access is required.
Soft-deletes the selected user. Administrator access is required.
Restores the selected soft-deleted user. Administrator access is required.
Permanently deletes the selected user. This action cannot be undone and requires administrator access.

item.description: Administrator-only user lifecycle operations.

}

request.description: Creates a project for company_id and stores its identifier in project_id. Administrator access is required. Valid statuses: active, pending, paused, completed, cancelled.
Returns any project, including its company and members. Administrator access is required.
Updates any project without requiring project membership. Administrator access is required.
Adds same-company users to any project. Valid roles: manager, member, viewer. Administrator access is required.
Removes the selected member from any project. Administrator access is required.
Soft-deletes any project. Administrator access is required.
Restores any soft-deleted project. Administrator access is required.
Permanently deletes any project. Administrator access is required and this action cannot be undone.

request.description: Creates a task in any project. Assignees must be members of the selected project.
Returns the selected accessible task with its project, assignees, dependencies, and notes.
Updates the selected task. Valid statuses: todo, in_progress, on_hold, blocked, review, done. Valid priorities: low, medium, high, emergency.
Updates only the task status. Valid values: todo, in_progress, on_hold, blocked, review, done. Assignees can update status; otherwise project management permission is required.

exec: pm.expect(assignee, 'authenticated user must be assigned to this task').to.be.an('object');

request.description: Updates the authenticated assignee progress fields on the task pivot. The authenticated user must be assigned to the task; task_seen and task_completed must render as JSON booleans.
Soft-deletes the selected task.
Restores the selected soft-deleted task.
Permanently deletes the task and its stored uploads. This action cannot be undone.

Removes the selected assignee from the task.
Adds same-project task dependencies. Self-dependencies and circular dependency chains are rejected.
Removes the selected dependency from the task.

Permanently deletes the selected project. The authenticated user must be a project manager. This action cannot be undone.

item.name: [4] Realtime

request.description: Open this as a Postman WebSocket request. Reverb speaks the Pusher protocol. After connecting and authorizing the private channel, send a subscribe message like {"event":"pusher:subscribe","data":{"channel":"private-chat.<authenticated_user_id>","auth":"<broadcast_auth>"}} and listen for .ai.message.received.

exec: pm.test('Private chat channel is authorized', function () {
const scope = pm.environment && pm.environment.name ? pm.environment : pm.collectionVariables;

request.description: Authorizes the authenticated Sanctum user for the private AI chat channel. Use a socket_id from the active WebSocket connection and channel_name private-chat.<authenticated_user_id>.

item.description: Laravel Reverb WebSocket connection and private channel authorization for AI chat events.
